commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Feishu streaming-card startup to pass the DM-aware `sendReplyToMessageId` and adds a dispatcher regression test for DM streaming cards.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +19. Total +19 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main computes a DM-aware `sendReplyToMessageId`, but streaming-card start still receives raw `replyToMessageId`, which selects Feishu reply mode. I did not run a live Feishu tenant reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are real Feishu proof or a maintainer proof override plus canonical selection among same-root PRs, not an automatable code repair.

target.title: fix(feishu): use sendReplyToMessageId in streaming card start() for DM chats
target.body:
Fixes #94922

## What

Change the streaming card `start()` call in `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` to use `sendReplyToMessageId` instead of the raw `replyToMessageId`.

## Why

In `reply-dispatcher.ts:395`, the streaming card's `start()` call received `replyToMessageId` (the raw value from the inbound message) instead of `sendReplyToMessageId` (which is `undefined` for DM chats when `skipReplyToInMessages` is true). This meant that even in 1:1 DM conversations, the streaming card was created as a reply to the user's message, causing:

1. A "Reply to [User]" label displayed in the Feishu client
2. Slower message delivery due to reply vs. direct send API overhead

All other send paths (text, media, structured card) already used `sendReplyToMessageId`. The streaming card was the only outlier.

The fix aligns the streaming card path with every other delivery path in the dispatcher, ensuring `skipReplyToInMessages` is respected consistently.

## Root cause

The `skipReplyToInMessages` guard (line 173) computes `sendReplyToMessageId` as `undefined` when `!isGroup && !directThreadReply` (DM sessions). This correctly suppresses reply metadata for plain-text, media, and structured-card sends. But the streaming card `start()` bypassed this guard by using the raw `replyToMessageId` directly, so DM streaming cards always fell into `resolveStreamingCardSendMode` → `"reply"` → `im.message.reply` API → visible "Reply to" label.

## Source-code alignment proof

This fix is a one-line change (`replyToMessageId` → `sendReplyToMessageId`) that aligns the streaming card start with **every existing send path** that already uses `sendReplyToMessageId`:

| Send path | File | Line | Uses `sendReplyToMessageId`? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plain-text final reply | `reply-dispatcher.ts` | ~523 | ✅ Yes |
| Media reply | `reply-dispatcher.ts` | ~523 | ✅ Yes |
| Structured card reply | `reply-dispatcher.ts` | ~523 | ✅ Yes |
| Streaming card start | `reply-dispatcher.ts` | 395 | ❌ **No (uses raw `replyToMessageId`)** → ✅ **Fixed in this PR** |

All non-streaming paths share the same `sendReplyToMessageId` argument through a unified send helper at line ~523. The streaming card `start()` was the only path that received the raw value — this PR corrects that single outlier.

### `resolveStreamingCardSendMode` contract

When `sendReplyToMessageId` is `undefined` (DM chats with `skipReplyToInMessages=true`), `resolveStreamingCardSendMode` returns `"create"` mode, which calls `im.message.create` (no Reply-to label). When it is present (group/thread), it returns `"reply"` mode, which calls `im.message.reply` (correct threaded behavior). Source: `extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts:194`.

### P2P direct-thread preservation

The `skipReplyToInMessages` guard (`!isGroup && !directThreadReply`) ensures direct-thread replies keep `sendReplyToMessageId` set, so they still use reply mode. This is tested in the existing test suite and preserved by PR #92136 (merged). This fix does not change that logic — it merely applies the *already-computed* `sendReplyToMessageId` to the streaming path.

### Regression test

A new test case `"omits replyToMessageId from streaming.start() in DM chats (skipReplyToInMessages)"` asserts that when `skipReplyToInMessages=true`, the streaming card receives `replyToMessageId=undefined`, causing `resolveStreamingCardSendMode` to return `"create"` mode.

## Request for maintainer proof override

This PR lacks live Feishu DM screenshots because we do not have Feishu enterprise credentials. However, the fix is strictly source-aligned: it applies the *same* `sendReplyToMessageId` guard that every other Feishu send path already uses, to the single path that was missing it. The behavior is determined entirely by `resolveStreamingCardSendMode`, which selects `"create"` vs `"reply"` based on the `replyToMessageId` argument — a function we can verify by source inspection.

A maintainer with Feishu credentials can override the proof gate by:
- Testing the fix in a real Feishu DM and confirming the Reply-to label is gone
- Posting `@openclaw-mantis visual task: verify in a real Feishu DM that a streaming card reply appears without a Reply-to label while a P2P direct-thread reply still stays threaded.`
- Or commenting `@clawsweeper re-review` after adding redacted live evidence

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: zoowh

commands.author: mraleko

target.title: feat(status): show session costs
target.body:
## Summary
- Use cumulative transcript-backed session cost for `/status` when `usage.cost.total` entries are available, so the status card shows session cost instead of a per-run snapshot.
- Add a conditional `Cost` column to `openclaw status` recent session rows when at least one shown session has transcript-backed cost.
- Update status/cost docs for the new behavior.

## Linked context
Fixes #64334.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
Behavior addressed: `/status` and `openclaw status` now surface cumulative transcript-backed session cost data.
Real environment tested: Local source checkout on macOS 26.5.1 arm64, Node 22.23.0, using an isolated temporary OpenClaw proof state with one synthetic `agent:main:main` session store and transcript. The proof state contained no real channel credentials, account identifiers, or user chat content.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created an isolated `$PROOF` home/state directory, populated the active `agent:main:main` transcript with two assistant usage records whose `usage.cost.total` values were `0.0021` and `0.0042`, started the local Gateway from commit `62216b2697` with a throwaway loopback token, sent `/status` through the documented Gateway WebSocket `connect` + `chat.send` RPC path, then read the resulting response through `chat.history`. Ran `openclaw status` against the same isolated state as a second proof path.
Evidence after fix: Copied slash `/status` output from the isolated gateway `chat.history` response:

```text
🦞 OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (62216b2)
⏱️ Uptime: gateway 24s · system 15d 9h
🧠 Model: openai/gpt-5.5
🧮 Tokens: 45 in / 32 out · 💵 Cost: $0.0063
📚 Context: 20/200k (0%) · 🧹 Compactions: 0
🧵 Session: agent:main:main • updated just now
⚙️ Execution: direct · Runtime: OpenClaw Default · Think: medium · Fast: off
🔌 Plugins: OK
🪢 Queue: steer (depth 0)
```

Supplemental real CLI output from the same isolated state showed the `openclaw status` Sessions table with the conditional `Cost` column:

```text
Sessions
┌──────────────────────────────────┬────────┬──────────┬─────────────────┬──────────────────┬────────────────┬─────────┐
│ Key                              │ Kind   │ Age      │ Model           │ Runtime          │ Tokens         │ Cost    │
├──────────────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────┼─────────────────┼──────────────────┼────────────────┼─────────┤
│ agent:main:main                  │ direct │ just now │ claude-opus-4-6 │ OpenClaw Default │ 0/1.0m (0%)    │ $0.0063 │
└──────────────────────────────────┴────────┴──────────┴─────────────────┴──────────────────┴────────────────┴─────────┘
```

Observed result after fix: Both changed user-visible status paths displayed `$0.0063`, matching the cumulative transcript usage costs `0.0021 + 0.0042` in the isolated proof state. The slash `/status` output showed `Cost: $0.0063`, and the CLI table showed `Cost` as `$0.0063`.
What was not tested: Real external messaging channels were not used for this proof; the slash-status evidence was captured from the local Gateway history in an isolated proof state. The full test suite was not run locally for this proof step.

## Additional verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/status.test.ts src/commands/status.command-sections.test.ts src/commands/status.command-report-data.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts`
- `PATH="/var/folders/6l/gn1ygj3d28s_pdrxjqkzc1440000gn/T/opencode/corepack-bin:$PATH" corepack pnpm check:docs`
- `PATH="/var/folders/6l/gn1ygj3d28s_pdrxjqkzc1440000gn/T/opencode/corepack-bin:$PATH" corepack pnpm format:check src/status/status-message.ts src/status/status-text.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts src/commands/status.command-sections.ts src/commands/status.command-report-data.ts src/commands/status.types.ts src/commands/status.command-sections.test.ts src/commands/status.command-report-data.test.ts src/commands/status.test-support.ts src/commands/status.test.ts docs/cli/status.md docs/reference/api-usage-costs.md docs/reference/token-use.md`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Known proof notes
- The branch was rebased onto current `origin/main` earlier in this PR.
- Global `corepack pnpm format:check` previously reported unrelated pre-existing formatting issues outside the touched files; the changed source/docs files pass the scoped formatter check listed above.
